paper.title: Post-cracking behaviour of steel and macro-synthetic fibre-reinforced concretes
paper.authors: Nicola Buratti; Claudio Mazzotti; Marco Savoia

evidence.evidence_text: Post-cracking behaviour of steel and macro-synthetic ﬁbre-reinforced concretes Nicola Buratti ⇑, Claudio Mazzotti, Marco Savoia DICAM-Structural Engineering, University of Bologna, Viale Risorgimento 2, 40136 Bologna, Italy article info Article history: Received 5 August 2010 Received in revised form 19 November 2010 Accepted 7 December 2010 Available online 7 January 2011 Keywords: Fibre-reinforced concrete Toughness Steel ﬁbres Macro-synthetic ﬁbres Three-point bending abstract The present pap
Cubes and a cylinder were cast to measure compressive strength and elastic modulus; packet states compressive strength is almost independent of fiber dosage and points to Fig. 5.

review_queue.question: Digitize or inspect the source figure/table to recover mixture-level compressive-strength values.
review_queue.suggested_action: Open the PDF page images and extract numeric strength values only if mixture labels, age, unit, and values can be confirmed.

main_risks: Compressive-strength values are not directly readable from packet text.
